hypothesis: Extending AMDEN to perform diffusion over the cell matrix C simultaneously with atomic positions X and element embeddings E (i.e., learning a joint diffusion process on (C, X, E) with appropriate equivariance and a density-regularization constraint) will enable generation of low-energy, density-targeted amorphous structures directly during inference, reducing or replacing the need for Hamiltonian Monte Carlo refinement while improving alignment with the energy distributions of annealed/quench datasets.
